spec_match.rationale: The concept page successfully maps out the security trust hierarchy across different origins for SVG, CSS, and backdrop filters.

interactive.rationale: The user can toggle between filter types and hover over rows to dynamically view specific rungs of the trust ladder.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Aligns cleanly with the feature description of blocked SVG filters on plugin and iframe boundary types.

edge_cases.rationale: It thoroughly covers sandbox status, srcdoc, plugin embeds, and data URIs which are all critical edge cases of this feature.

references.rationale: The see also section now includes the required chromestatus.com/feature/5117170452398080 reference link.

design_system.rationale: The trust ladder rows are now keyboard-focusable disclosure controls with role, aria-controls, aria-expanded, visible focus styling, and Enter/Space toggling.

summary: DevTools MCP reproduced the original gap at http://localhost:3000/v150/disable-svg-filters-on-plugins-and-cross-origin-or-restricted-iframes/origin-trust-ladder/?verify=before-keyboard-details: all six ladder rows had tabIndex -1, no role, no aria-expanded state, and focusing them did not reveal details. The repaired page was tested at /origin-trust-ladder/?verify=after-keyboard-details in Chrome 148: the accessibility tree exposed all six rows as expandable buttons, Enter opened the cross-origin row detail, Space collapsed it, CSS filter switching updated aria-pressed button state and row labels, and a keyboard toggle opened the plugin-embed rationale. Inline event handlers were removed, the page kept its ChromeStatus link, and both the concept page and conformance page had no console errors or failed network requests. The feature conformance route ran at /v150/disable-svg-filters-on-plugins-and-cross-origin-or-restricted-iframes/conformance/?verify=origin-trust-ladder-keyboard with 5 PASS / 0 FAIL / 5 TOTAL.
